KXCPI-26MAY-T0.4.reason: May's 0.5% MoM was energy-driven (+3.9%); with energy prices likely stabilizing, June CPI expected ~0.3%, making >0.4% less probable than market implies.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.5.reason: May energy spike unlikely to repeat in June; core CPI at +0.2% MoM signals contained underlying inflation, reducing odds of another 0.5%+ print.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.6.reason: In line with market; would require a second consecutive large energy shock or an unexpected food surge on top of sticky shelter costs.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.7.reason: Very low probability; compounding energy and shelter acceleration simultaneously is a tail scenario inconsistent with current softening core trends.

KXCPI-26MAY-T0.8.reason: Extreme tail only; a 0.8%+ monthly CPI print would match March 2022 levels and is incompatible with the current benign core and moderating food trajectory.
